questions.question: Define Machine Learning and explain its relationship with Artificial Intelligence.
keyPoints: ML is a subset of AI that enables systems to learn from data
ML focuses on developing algorithms that learn from data without explicit programming
AI is the broader field encompassing various approaches to create intelligent systems

questions.question: Differentiate between Supervised, Unsupervised, and Reinforcement Learning with one example each.
keyPoints: Supervised: Learning from labeled data (e.g., spam detection)
Unsupervised: Learning from unlabeled data (e.g., customer segmentation)
Reinforcement: Learning through rewards/penalties (e.g., game-playing AI)

questions.question: What is overfitting? How can it be prevented?
keyPoints: Overfitting: Model learns training data too well including noise
Results in poor generalization to new data
Prevention: Regularization, cross-validation, reducing model complexity, more training data

questions.question: Explain the concept of bias-variance tradeoff in machine learning.
keyPoints: Bias: Error from overly simple model (underfitting)
Variance: Error from model being too sensitive to training data (overfitting)
Goal: Find optimal balance to minimize total error and achieve good generalization

questions.question: What is feature engineering? Why is it important in ML?
keyPoints: Feature engineering: Process of selecting and transforming raw data into meaningful features
Helps ML algorithms learn patterns more effectively
Improves model performance and prediction accuracy

questions.question: Distinguish between classification and regression problems in ML.
keyPoints: Classification: Predicts discrete categories/labels (e.g., spam/not spam)
Regression: Predicts continuous numerical values (e.g., house prices)
Both are supervised learning techniques

questions.question: What is the purpose of splitting data into training and test sets?
keyPoints: Training set: Used to train/fit the model
Test set: Used to evaluate model performance on unseen data
Ensures model can generalize beyond training data

questions.question: Explain the three main types of machine learning. Discuss their characteristics, differences, and provide real-world applications for each type.
keyPoints: Supervised Learning: Uses labeled data, learns input-output mapping, examples include classification and regression, applications in email filtering, medical diagnosis
Unsupervised Learning: Uses unlabeled data, finds hidden patterns/structures, examples include clustering and dimensionality reduction, applications in customer segmentation, anomaly detection
Reinforcement Learning: Agent learns through interaction with environment receiving rewards/penalties, used in game AI, robotics, autonomous systems
Key difference: Presence/absence of labels and learning mechanism

questions.question: Define overfitting and underfitting. Explain how to identify these problems and discuss techniques to address them in machine learning models.
keyPoints: Underfitting: Model too simple, high bias, poor performance on both train and test data
Overfitting: Model too complex, high variance, excellent train performance but poor test performance
Identification: Compare training and testing errors
Solutions for underfitting: Increase model complexity, add more features, train longer
Solutions for overfitting: Regularization, cross-validation, dropout, reduce complexity, get more data
The bias-variance tradeoff illustrates the balance needed

questions.question: What is a neural network? Explain its basic architecture and how deep learning extends traditional machine learning approaches.
keyPoints: Neural Network: Computational model inspired by biological neurons
Architecture: Input layer, hidden layers, output layer with interconnected nodes/neurons
Deep Learning: Neural networks with multiple hidden layers enabling hierarchical feature learning
Advantages: Can learn complex patterns automatically, handles raw data like images/text
Comparison: Traditional ML requires feature engineering; DL learns features automatically
Applications: Image recognition, NLP, speech recognition
